title: Uruguayan Lamb Empanadas
description: Tender lamb empanadas with gooey cheese filling, perfect for a crowd.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine ground lamb, onion, garlic, salt, and pepper. Mix well with your hands until just combined.
In a separate bowl, mix together shredded cheddar cheese and egg.
Add the cheese mixture to the lamb mixture and mix until just combined.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 12-15 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Use a rolling pin to flatten each ball of dough into a thin circle.
Spoon about 1 tablespoon of the lamb mixture onto the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough in half over the filling and press edges together with a fork to seal.
Heat about 1/4 inch of olive oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat.
When the oil is hot, add a few empanadas to the pan and cook for 3-4 minutes on each side, or until golden brown.
Serve hot with a side of baby lettuce leaves.
